title: Breitmann As An Uhlan. II. Breitmann in a Balloon
content:
آ آ آ آ Who vas efer hear soosh voonders,
آ آ آ آ Holy breest or virshin nonn?
آ آ آ آ As pefelled de Coptain Breitmann,
آ آ آ آ Vhen he hoont an air-ballon.
آ آ آ آ Der Bizzy und der Dizzy,
آ آ آ آ Mit lothairingen und Lothair,
آ آ آ آ Vas nodings to dis Deutscher,
آ آ آ آ Who vent kitin troo de air.

آ آ آ آ Id was in yar Nofember,
آ آ آ آ In eighdeen sefendee,
آ آ آ آ Der Breitmann vent a prowlin,
آ آ آ آ By monden light vent he.
آ آ آ آ In fillages deserted
آ آ آ آ He hear de Uhu moan;
آ آ آ آ For you alvays hear der Uhu
آ آ آ آ Vhere der Uhu-lan ish gone.

آ آ آ آ Alone allonsed der Uhlan,
آ آ آ آ Boot nodings could he find
آ آ آ آ Safe whitey clouds a drivin
آ آ آ آ In moonshine fore de wind.
آ آ آ آ Boot ash he see dese cloudins
آ آ آ آ He bemark dat von vas round,
آ آ آ آ Und inshtead of goin oopwarts
آ آ آ آ It kep risin towards de ground.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Oh, vot ish dis a gomin?
آ آ آ آ Some planet, py de Lord!
آ آ آ آ Too boor to life in heafen,
آ آ آ آ Coom down on eart to poard;
آ آ آ آ Und pelow it schwing tree engelsï؟½
آ آ آ آ Two he-vons mit a wench.
آ آ آ آ Boot, mein Gott! vot sort of engels
آ آ آ آ Can dose pe, dalkin Frï؟½ntsch!

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I hafe read in Eckhartshausen
آ آ آ آ Dat oop in heafenï؟½py tam!
آ آ آ آ De engels dalk in Sherman,
آ آ آ آ Und sing Mardin Lutherï؟½s psalm.
آ آ آ آ O neinï؟½es sind kein engeln
آ آ آ آ Vot sail so smoofly on,
آ آ آ آ Das sind verfluchte Franzosen
آ آ آ آ In einem luft-ballon!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Hei! how der Breitman streak it
آ آ آ آ Ven vonce he kess de trutï؟½!
آ آ آ آ He spurred id like de wild fire
آ آ آ آ Of hope in early youtï؟½.
آ آ آ آ Troo de weingarts like der teufel
آ آ آ آ Vhen he shase a lawyerï؟½s soul;
آ آ آ آ Down der moundain mit his lanze
آ آ آ آ Und his wafin banderol.

آ آ آ آ Down de moundain, oï؟½er de valley,
آ آ آ آ Troo de village he ish gone;
آ آ آ آ Dog-barks die out pehind him,
آ آ آ آ Oders bark ash he come on.
آ آ آ آ Liddle heedet he deir bellin,
آ آ آ آ Liddle mind der Hahnen crow;
آ آ آ آ Liddle hear der Bauern yellin,
آ آ آ آ Clotter, clodder, on he go.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Oh, vot ish hoontin foxen,
آ آ آ آ Und vot ish yï؟½ger pliss,
آ آ آ آ Und vot ish shasin bison
آ آ آ آ On de blains, to soosh ash dis?
آ آ آ آ I hafe dinked dat roonin rebels
آ آ آ آ Vas de best of eartly fun;
آ آ آ آ Boot id isnï؟½t half so sholly
آ آ آ آ Ash to go a luft-ballon.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Und ash id shdill vent onwart,
آ آ آ آ Shdill onwarts mit der wind,
آ آ آ آ Der coom a real madness
آ آ آ آ To catch id, oï؟½er his mind.
آ آ آ آ Und hadï؟½st dou seen him vylin,
آ آ آ آ Dat wild onfuriate brick,
آ آ آ آ Douï؟½st hafe schworn dat Coptain Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Was pecome balloonatic.

آ آ آ آ In fain dey trow deir sand-bags,
آ آ آ آ In fain all dings let fall,
آ آ آ آ De ballon shdill kep a sinkin,
آ آ آ آ Und id vouldnï؟½t rise at all.
آ آ آ آ Yet de wild wind trife id onwarts,
آ آ آ آ Onwarts shdill der Breitmann go,
آ آ آ آ Dill he cotch id py a rope-ent
آ آ آ آ Vot vas hangin town pelow.

آ آ آ آ Boot vhen it risen oopwarts,
آ آ آ آ Ash he cling to id, of corse,
آ آ آ آ Mit de lefter hand he holtet
آ آ آ آ To de pridle of his horse.
آ آ آ آ Der horse valk on his hind-legs:
آ آ آ آ Too schwer to rise vas he;
آ آ آ آ Mein Gott! vot fix for Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Of de Uhlan cavallrie!

آ آ آ آ So he go for seferal stunden
آ آ آ آ Petween himmel und eart pelow,
آ آ آ آ Boot der teufel und die engels
آ آ آ آ Couldnï؟½t make der Hans let go.
آ آ آ آ Dill all at vonce an idï؟½e
آ آ آ آ Coom from his loocky shtarï؟½
آ آ آ آ He led co his horseï؟½s pridle
آ آ آ آ Und glimb oop indo de car.

آ آ آ آ Und vot you dinks he foundet
آ آ آ آ Vhen in dat air-ballon?
آ آ آ آ A nople Englisch vicomte,
آ آ آ آ Milord de Robinson;
آ آ آ آ Und mit him vas a laity,
آ آ آ آ Mit whom heï؟½d rooned afay,
آ آ آ آ Whom he indroduce to Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Ash die Jungfer Salomï؟½.

آ آ آ آ Und der dritte was a barson,
آ آ آ آ Whom Milord, mit prudent view,
آ آ آ آ Hat took als secretairï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Likevise for pallast doo.
آ آ آ آ Dey should hafe bitched him ofer
آ آ آ آ Vhen de gas was out, dey say;
آ آ آ آ Boot de damï؟½ vould not ï؟½low it:ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Sheï؟½d an arriï؟½re pensï؟½e.

آ آ آ آ Sait Milord: ï؟½Afar weï؟½ve wandered,
آ آ آ آ We are completely brown;
آ آ آ آ And Iï؟½ll give a thousand shiners
آ آ آ آ If youï؟½ll take me to a town
آ آ آ آ Where no one will molest us
آ آ آ آ Till we find our way to Lonï؟½ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Here der Breitmann ent de sentence
آ آ آ آ Ash he gry out, shortly, ï؟½done.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ ï؟½And as for this fair lady
آ آ آ آ To whom I would be bound,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Sait Milord, ï؟½weï؟½ll have a wedding
آ آ آ آ Before we reach the ground.
آ آ آ آ To escape her fatherï؟½s anger
آ آ آ آ We fled to live in peace,
آ آ آ آ But sheï؟½s relatives in London,
آ آ آ آ And they haveï؟½the police.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ O vas not dis a voonders
آ آ آ آ To make de Captain shdare?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ A tausend pounds in bocket
آ آ آ آ Und a veddin in de air?
آ آ آ آ He gafe avay de laity,
آ آ آ آ Und als sie wieder kam
آ آ آ آ Zur festen Erde wieder,
آ آ آ آ Ward sie Robinson Madame.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½O go mit me,ï؟½ said Breitmann,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½O go in mein Quartier!
آ آ آ آ Donï؟½t mind dem gommon soldiers,
آ آ آ آ For Iï؟½m an officier.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ He guide dem troo de coontry
آ آ آ آ Till dey reach de ocean strand;
آ آ آ آ Now dey sit und pless Hans Breitmann,
آ آ آ آ In de far-off English land.

آ آ آ آ Dis ish Breitmannï؟½s last adfenture
آ آ آ آ How troo Himmel air flew he:
آ آ آ آ Und itï؟½s dime, oh nople reader!
آ آ آ آ For a dime to part from dee.
آ آ آ آ Dou mayï؟½st dake it all in earnest
آ آ آ آ Or pelieve idï؟½s only fon;
آ آ آ آ Boot dereï؟½s woonder dings has hoppent
آ آ آ آ Fery oft in Luft-ballon.
meaning:
About: Charles Godfrey Leland was an American humorist and folklorist, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. He was educated at Princeton University and in Europe.

Leland worked in journalism, travelled extensively, and became interested in folklore and folk linguistics, publishing books and articles on American and European languages and folk traditions. Leland worked in a wide variety of trades, achieved recognition as the author of the comic Hans Breitmannï؟½s Ballads, fought in two conflicts, and wrote what was to become a primary source text for Neopaganism half a century later, Aradia, or the Gospel of the Witches.


Source:- Wikipedia.
